test.observation: All tests passed. Note: Project has 4 pre-existing unrelated test failures in chat-history-pagination.test.ts, db-env-guard.test.ts, and esco.test.ts that are not related to this feature.

typecheck.observation: Pre-existing TypeScript errors from libSQL/Turso migration (execute method, embedding column types). No new type errors introduced by scoring.ts or quality signals implementation.

lint.observation: Pre-existing Biome issues: unused import in genui-utils.ts, any type usage in golden-queries test, dangerouslySetInnerHTML in layout.tsx. No linting issues introduced by scoring.ts or .env.example changes.

appliedUpdates.description: Added QUALITY_SIGNAL_* environment variables documentation following same pattern as RECENCY_* vars with clear Dutch comments explaining each variable's purpose and default value.
Removed .factory/library/environment.md which contained mission-specific recency/quality documentation that was not reusable across other missions.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that candidates.lastMatchedAt field is available for scoring logic. Workers should verify existing schema fields against src/db/schema.ts before assuming field availability.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Recency scoring used lastMatchedAt successfully; review flagged this as knowledge gap initially but field exists in schema

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance that environment variable documentation must go in .env.example, not in shared library. Shared library should only contain cross-mission reusable knowledge patterns and conventions.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Mission-specific env var documentation in .factory/library/environment.md was incorrectly placed and had to be removed

rejectedObservations.observation: QUALITY_CONFIG export pattern differs slightly from RECENCY_CONFIG in naming convention
rejectedObservations.reason: Both follow consistent PascalCase pattern used elsewhere in project. QUALITY_CONFIG is correct and matches existing convention.

rejectedObservations.observation: Quality reasoning uses Dutch strings while some workers prefer English for code
rejectedObservations.reason: Dutch UI strings with English code is established project convention documented in AGENTS.md. Quality reasoning correctly uses Dutch ('Hoge goedkeuring', 'Lage goedkeuring').
